Title: Outcomes of total knee arthroplasty in elderly patients
Abstract: Background: Total knee arthroplasty (TKA) is one of the most commonly performed surgical procedures. This study evaluated outcomes in patients over 75 years of age. Methods: A retrospective cohort study of 250 patients. Results: Significant improvement in WOMAC scores at 12 months. Conclusion: TKA is effective and safe in elderly patients.
Authors: Smith J, Johnson A, Williams B

Title: Robotic-assisted vs conventional knee replacement: a systematic review
Abstract: This systematic review compared robotic-assisted total knee arthroplasty with conventional techniques. We searched PubMed, EMBASE, and Cochrane databases. Twenty-three RCTs were included. Robotic assistance showed improved alignment but similar clinical outcomes at 2 years.
Authors: Chen X, Lee M, Park S

Title: Physical therapy protocols after TKA: current evidence
Abstract: We reviewed current evidence on physical therapy protocols following total knee arthroplasty. Early mobilization and continuous passive motion showed benefits in range of motion recovery. Telehealth-based PT showed equivalent outcomes to in-person sessions.

Title: Patient satisfaction and quality of life after joint replacement
Abstract: A prospective study of 500 patients undergoing hip or knee replacement. Patient-reported outcome measures (PROMs) were collected at baseline, 6 months, and 12 months. Overall satisfaction rate was 89%. Younger patients reported higher expectations but similar satisfaction.
Authors: Davis L, Wilson M, Brown T

Title: Cost-effectiveness of unicompartmental vs total knee arthroplasty
Abstract: Economic evaluation comparing unicompartmental knee arthroplasty (UKA) with TKA for medial compartment osteoarthritis. UKA showed lower initial costs and faster recovery. At 10 years, revision rates were higher for UKA but overall cost-effectiveness was similar.

Title: Perioperative pain management in knee arthroplasty
Abstract: Multimodal analgesia protocols for TKA were compared in this network meta-analysis. Adductor canal blocks combined with periarticular injection provided optimal pain control. Opioid consumption was significantly reduced with multimodal approaches.
Authors: Thompson E, White C, Harris D

Title: Infection prevention strategies in joint replacement surgery
Abstract: Review of current evidence on surgical site infection prevention in arthroplasty. Laminar airflow, antibiotic-loaded cement, and standardized skin preparation protocols were evaluated. Bundle approaches reduced infection rates by 40%.

Title: Long-term outcomes of cementless total knee arthroplasty
Abstract: Ten-year follow-up study of 300 cementless TKA procedures. Survivorship was 96.2% at 10 years. Radiolucent lines were present in 12% of cases but were non-progressive. Functional outcomes were comparable to cemented implants.

Title: The effect of BMI on total knee arthroplasty outcomes
Abstract: Meta-analysis of 45 studies examining the impact of body mass index on TKA outcomes. Morbid obesity (BMI>40) was associated with higher complication rates and lower functional scores. Moderate obesity showed acceptable outcomes with appropriate patient selection.
Authors: O'Brien K, Murphy J, Kelly P

Title: Blood management strategies in bilateral knee arthroplasty
Abstract: Prospective randomized trial comparing tranexamic acid protocols in simultaneous bilateral TKA. Intravenous plus topical TXA reduced blood loss by 35% compared to IV alone. No increased thromboembolic events were observed.
